message:
🌱 Add governor watchdog to auto-restart dead governor timer (#73)

Separate systemd timer (every 10 min) checks if kick-governor.timer is
active. If not, re-enables it and logs via syslog. Prevents the governor
from staying dead indefinitely — no agent can detect or fix this since
they all depend on the governor to kick them.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+[Unit]
+Description=Governor watchdog — re-enables kick-governor.timer if it goes inactive
+
+[Service]
+Type=oneshot
+ExecStart=/bin/bash -c 'if ! systemctl is-active --quiet kick-governor.timer; then systemctl enable --now kick-governor.timer && logger -t governor-watchdog "re-enabled kick-governor.timer"; fi'
